Notes.Text:
This update for cosign fixes the following issues

Security issue:

- CVE-2026-39395: Incorrect attestation verification due to malformed payloads or mismatched predicate types
  (bsc#1261859).

Non security issue:

- Update to version 3.0.5 (jsc#SLE-23879).
Notes.Title: Details

Notes.Text: The CVRF data is provided by SUSE under the Creative Commons License 4.0 with Attribution (CC-BY-4.0).
Notes.Title: Terms of Use

Relationships.RelatesToProductReference: SUSE Linux Enterprise Server for SAP applications 16.0

Vulnerabilities.Description: Cosign provides code signing and transparency for containers and binaries. Prior to 3.0.6 and 2.6.3, cosign verify-blob-attestation may erroneously report a "Verified OK" result for attestations with malformed payloads or mismatched predicate types. For old-format bundles and detached signatures, this was due to a logic flaw in the error handling of the predicate type validation. For new-format bundles, the predicate type validation was bypassed completely. This vulnerability is fixed in 3.0.6 and 2.6.3.

ProductID: SUSE Linux Enterprise Server for SAP applications 16.0:cosign-3.0.6-160000.1.1
